Subject: Accounting 2nd Paper
Stem:
The Receipts and Payments Account of Khajanagar Club for the year ended 31st December 2018 is given below:

Khajanagar Club
Receipts and Payments Account
For the year ended 31st December 2018
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Receipts | Amount (Tk.) | Payments | Amount (Tk.)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Opening Balance: | | Salary (3/4) | 18,000
   Cash | 5,600 | Rent | 12,000
   Bank Deposit | 18,500 -> 24,100 | 10% Investment (01-07-2018) | 50,000
Subscription: | | Sports Equipment | 9,000
   2017 | 4,500 | Stationery | 7,000
   2018 | 41,500 -> 46,000 | Closing Balance (31-12-2018): |
Interest on Investment | 800 |    Cash | 9,100
Donation | 45,000 |    Bank Deposit | 10,000 -> 19,100
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total | 1,15,900 | Total | 1,15,900
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

On 1st January 2018, other assets of the club were: Sports Equipment Tk. 60,000 and Furniture Tk. 35,000.

Other Information:
(1) Total number of club members is 400. The subscription rate is Tk. 100 per member.
(2) 2/5th of the received donation is to be used to form a Poor Welfare Fund, and the remaining portion is to be treated as revenue income.
(3) Charge 10% depreciation on the closing balance of Furniture and Sports Equipment.
(4) Out of the subscription collected during the current year, Tk. 3,000 is received in advance for the next year.
Question_A: Determine the amount of outstanding subscription for the current year.
Question_B: Prepare the Income and Expenditure Account of the club for the year ended 31st December 2018.
Question_C: What is the amount of total assets of the club on the given date?
Ans_A:
Determination of Outstanding Subscription for the Current Year:

-----------------------------------------------------------------
Particulars | Details (Tk.) | Amount (Tk.)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Total subscription due for current year (400 members × Tk. 100) | | 40,000
Subscription received for 2018 (given in receipts) | 41,500 | 
(-) Advance received for next year included in it | (3,000) | 
--------------------------------------------------|---------|
Net subscription received for current year | | (38,500)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Outstanding Subscription for Current Year | | 1,500
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Ans_B:
Khajanagar Club
Income and Expenditure Account
For the year ended 31st December 2018

-----------------------------------------------------------------
Expenditure | Details (Tk.) | Amount (Tk.) | Income | Details (Tk.) | Amount (Tk.)
-----------------------------------------------------------------
Salary | 18,000 | | Subscription | 41,500 | 
(+) Outstanding (Note 2) | 6,000 | 24,000 | (-) Advance | (3,000) | 
| | | (+) Outstanding | 1,500 | 40,000
Rent | | 12,000 | | | 
Stationery | | 7,000 | Donation (Revenue portion: 3/5) | 45,000 × 3/5 | 27,000
Depreciation on Furniture | 35,000 × 10% | 3,500 | Interest on Investment | 800 | 
Depreciation on Sports Eq. | (60k+9k) × 10% | 6,900 | (+) Accrued Interest (Note 1) | 1,700 | 2,500
Surplus of Income over Exp. | | 7,100 | | | 
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total | | 60,500 | Total | | 60,500
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Working Notes:
1. Accrued Interest on Investment: Total interest = Tk. 50,000 × 10% × (6/12) = Tk. 2,500. Received interest = Tk. 800. Therefore, Accrued Interest = Tk. 2,500 - Tk. 800 = Tk. 1,700.
2. Outstanding Salary: Tk. 18,000 represents 3/4th of the salary. Total salary = Tk. 18,000 × (4/3) = Tk. 24,000. Outstanding amount = Tk. 24,000 - Tk. 18,000 = Tk. 6,000.

Other Information:

ii. Fees receivable from patient visits for the current year amounts to Tk. 1,85,000.
iii. Out of the amount paid for the purchase of medicine, Tk. 15,000 belongs to the previous year's outstanding balance. On the other hand, Tk. 4,000 for the current year's medicine purchase remains unpaid/outstanding.
iv. Ambulance repair and servicing bill payable/outstanding is Tk. 10,000.
v. Depreciation is to be charged on Furniture at the rate of 10%.

* Working Note for Opening Investment:
Interest on investment received during the year is Tk. 15,000. Since a new investment of Tk. 80,000 was made on 01-07-2018, the interest on this new investment for 6 months is: Tk. 80,000 × 15% × (6/12) = Tk. 6,000.
Therefore, interest from the opening investment = Tk. 15,000 - Tk. 6,000 = Tk. 9,000.
Hence, Opening Investment = (Tk. 9,000 × 100) / 15 = Tk. 60,000.

On 1st January 2017, the assets of the club were: 10% Investment Taka 1,000,000, Building Taka 500,000, Furniture Taka 50,000, Sports Equipment Taka 10,000.

**Other Information:**
i. Outstanding subscription for the current year is Taka 2,000.
ii. Outstanding salary and outstanding wages for the current year are Taka 500 and Taka 700 respectively.
iii. Rent has been paid in advance by Taka 2,500.

Question_A: Determine the amount of subscription for the current year.
Question_B: Determine the amount of opening capital fund.

**Determination of Subscription for the Current Year:**

| Add: Outstanding subscription for current year | 2,000 | |

| | | Building | 5,00,000 |

| | | Sports Equipment | 10,000 |
| | | Outstanding Subscription of Previous Year (2016) | 3,000 |
| **Total** | **7,03,000** | **Total** | **7,03,000** |

Therefore, the Opening Capital Fund is Taka 7,03,000.
Ans_C:
**Revive Club**
**Income and Expenditure Account**
**For the year ended 31st December 2017**

| Expenditure | Details Taka | Amount Taka | Income | Details Taka | Amount Taka |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Revenue Expenditure:** | | | **Revenue Income:** | | |
| Salary | 15,000 | | Admission Fees | | 15,000 |
| Add: Current year's outstanding | 500 | 15,500 | Subscription (for 2017) | | 62,000 |
| Wages | 20,000 | | Interest on Investment | 5,000 | |
| Add: Current year's outstanding | 700 | 20,700 | Add: Accrued Interest | 5,000 | 10,000 |
| Repairs and Renewals | | 5,000 | Sale of Drama Tickets | | 25,000 |
| Drama Performance Expenses | | 15,000 | Sale of Old Newspapers | | 1,000 |
| Rent | 20,000 | | | | |
| Less: Advance paid | (2,500) | 17,500 | | | |
| Stationery | 2,000 | | | | |
| Less: Unused | (500) | 1,500 | | | |

| **Total** | | **1,13,000** | **Total** | | **1,13,000** |

**Working Notes:**
1. **Accrued Interest on Investment:** Total interest due = Taka 1,00,000 × 10% = Taka 10,000. Interest received = Taka 5,000. Therefore, Accrued Interest = Taka 10,000 - Taka 5,000 = Taka 5,000.
2. **Depreciation on Building:** Taka 5,00,000 × 2% = Taka 10,000.
3. **Depreciation on Sports Equipment:** Taka 10,000 × 20% = Taka 2,000.
4. **Depreciation on Furniture:** On opening balance (Taka 50,000 × 10%) = Taka 5,000. On new purchase (Taka 30,000 × 10% × 6/12) = Taka 1,500. Total Depreciation = Taka 5,000 + Taka 1,500 = Taka 6,500.

Stem:
The Receipts and Payments Account of Sayem Smriti Sangsad for the year ended 31st December 2018 is given below:

**Sayem Smriti Sangsad**
**Receipts and Payments Account**
**For the year ended 31st December 2018**

| Receipts | Taka | Payments | Taka |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Opening Cash Balance | 9,500 | Salary | 18,000 |
| Subscription | 41,500 | Entertainment Expenses | 16,500 |
| Receipts from Entertainment | 37,300 | Stationery Expenses | 1,500 |
| Legacies | 11,200 | Purchase of Billiard Table (01-07-2018) | 17,000 |
| Donation | 20,000 | Purchase of Furniture (30-06-2018) | 20,000 |
| Sale of Old Furniture (Cost Taka 6,000) | 5,500 | Wages of Groundkeeper | 6,500 |
| | | Pavilion Development | 25,000 |
| | | Purchase of Books | 10,000 |
| | | Closing Cash Balance | 10,500 |
| **Total** | **1,25,000** | **Total** | **1,25,000** |

The opening assets of the club were as follows: Billiard Table Taka 30,000, Furniture Taka 25,000, Food/Stock for entertainment Taka 5,300, Outstanding Subscription Taka 2,800.

**Other Information:**
i. Taka 1,300 remains outstanding for entertainment foodstuffs.
ii. Outstanding subscription for the current year is Taka 2,100.
iii. 10% depreciation is to be charged on fixed assets.
Question_A: Determine the amount of profit or loss from entertainment.
Question_B: Determine the amount of opening capital fund.
Question_C: Prepare the Income and Expenditure Account.
Ans_A:
**Determination of Profit/Loss from Entertainment:**

| Particulars | Details Taka | Amount Taka |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Receipts from Entertainment | | 37,300 |
| **Less: Cost of Entertainment Consumption:** | | |
| Opening stock of entertainment foodstuffs | 5,300 | |
| Add: Entertainment expenses paid during the year | 16,500 | |
| Add: Outstanding entertainment expenses | 1,300 | |
| Total entertainment cost incurred | | (23,100) |
| **Profit from Entertainment** | | **14,200** |
Ans_B:
**Determination of Opening Capital Fund (as on 01-01-2018):**

| Liabilities | Taka | Assets | Taka |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Capital Fund (Balancing figure) | 72,600 | Opening Cash Balance | 9,500 |
| | | Billiard Table | 30,000 |
| | | Furniture | 25,000 |
| | | Opening Stock of Entertainment Foodstuffs | 5,300 |
| | | Outstanding Subscription of Previous Year | 2,800 |
| **Total** | **72,600** | **Total** | **72,600** |

Therefore, the Opening Capital Fund is Taka 72,600.
Ans_C:
**Sayem Smriti Sangsad**
**Income and Expenditure Account**
**For the year ended 31st December 2018**

| Expenditure | Details Taka | Amount Taka | Income | Details Taka | Amount Taka |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Salary | | 18,000 | Subscription | 41,500 | |
| Stationery Expenses | | 1,500 | Less: Previous year's outstanding | (2,800) | |
| Wages of Groundkeeper | | 6,500 | Current year's collected subscription | 38,700 | |
| **Depreciation:** | | | Add: Current year's outstanding | 2,100 | 40,800 |
| On Billiard Table (Calculation 1) | | 3,850 | Profit from Entertainment (from Part A) | | 14,200 |
| On Furniture (Calculation 2) | | 2,900 | | | |
| On Books ($10,000 \times 10\%$) | | 1,000 | | | |
| Loss on Sale of Furniture (Calculation 3) | | 500 | | | |
| Surplus (Excess of Income over Expenditure) | | 20,750 | | | |
| **Total** | | **55,000** | **Total** | | **55,000** |

**Calculations & Working Notes:**
1. **Depreciation on Billiard Table:** On opening balance ($30,000 \times 10\% = 3,000$). On new purchase ($17,000 \times 10\% \times \frac{6}{12} = 850$). Total = Taka 3,850.
2. **Depreciation on Furniture:** The cost of the sold portion is Taka 6,000. So, depreciation on the remaining opening balance ($[25,000 - 6,000] \times 10\% = 1,900$). On new purchase ($20,000 \times 10\% \times \frac{6}{12} = 1,000$). Total = Taka 2,900.
3. **Loss on Sale of Furniture:** Cost price is Taka 6,000 and sale price is Taka 5,500. So, Loss = $6,000 - 5,500 =$ Taka 500.
4. **Pavilion Development:** It represents an expansion of a landmark/landed facility, which is a capital expenditure asset and therefore excluded from revenue expenses.

Subject: Accounting 2nd Paper
Level: Dhaka Cantonment Girls' Public School & College
Stem:
The Receipts and Payments Account of Uttara Club for the year ended June 30, 2026, and other information are given below:

Uttara Club
Receipts and Payments Account
Debit Side (Receipts): Balance (01-07-2024) 9,030; Subscription 30,000; Entrance Fee 500; Donation 10,000; 10% Loan (taken 01-09-2024) 12,000; Sale of old periodicals 8,470. Total Debit = 70,000.
Credit Side (Payments): Rent 17,800; Stationery 2,530; Electricity expense 2,070; Loan interest paid 800; Purchase of sports equipment 14,000; Purchase of books 15,500; Entertainment expenses 1,610; Balance (30-06-2025) 15,690. Total Credit = 70,000.
Additional Information: 1. Assets on July 1, 2024: Furniture 30,000, Books 40,000. 2. Last year's rent 300 was paid this year, while current year's rent 500 is still unpaid. 3. Current year's subscription outstanding is 3,000, while next year's subscription received in advance is 1,500. 4. 10% depreciation must be charged on the closing balance of furniture.
Question_A: Calculate the amount of the opening Capital Fund.
Question_B: Prepare an Income & Expenditure Account for the year ended June 30, 2025.
Question_C: Prepare a Balance Sheet as of June 30, 2025.
Ans_A:
Calculation of Opening Capital Fund:

Opening Assets:
Cash in hand (01-07-2024) = 9,030
Furniture = 30,000
Books = 40,000
Total Opening Assets = (9,030 + 30,000 + 40,000) = 79,030

Less: Opening Liabilities:
Outstanding Rent of last year = 300

Opening Capital Fund = (79,030 - 300) = 78,730
Ans_B:
Uttara Club
Income and Expenditure Account
For the year ended June 30, 2025
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Income:
Subscription (30,000 + Outstanding 3,000 - Advance 1,500) = 31,500
Entrance Fee = 500
Sale of old periodicals = 8,470
Total Income = 40,470

Less: Expenditure:
Rent (17,800 - Last year's 300 + Current year's 500) = 18,000
Stationery = 2,530
Electricity Expense = 2,070
Loan Interest (Paid 800 + Accrued 200) = 1,000 [Note: 10% interest on 12,000 for 10 months = 1,000]
Depreciation on Furniture (30,000 x 10%) = 3,000
Entertainment Expenses = 1,610
Total Expenditure = 28,210

Surplus (Excess of Income over Expenditure) = (40,470 - 28,210) = 12,260
Ans_C:
Uttara Club
Balance Sheet
As of June 30, 2025
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Assets:
Current Assets: Cash in hand 15,690, Outstanding Subscription 3,000. (Total Current Assets = 18,690)
Fixed Assets: Furniture (30,000 - Depreciation 3,000) = 27,000; Sports Equipment = 14,000; Books (40,000 + Purchase 15,500) = 55,500. (Total Fixed Assets = 96,500)
Total Assets = (18,690 + 96,500) = 1,15,190

Liabilities & Capital Fund:
Current Liabilities: Outstanding Rent 500, Advance Subscription 1,500, Accrued Loan Interest 200. (Total Current Liabilities = 2,200)
Long-term Liabilities: 10% Loan = 12,000
Capital Fund: Opening Capital 78,730 + Surplus 12,260 + Donation 10,000 = 1,00,990
Total Liabilities & Capital Fund = (2,200 + 12,000 + 1,00,990) = 1,15,190
